variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Original curved body shell. Floor mats & seat covers completely unique to this phase.

variants.name: 2nd Generation
variants.notes: Longer wheelbase. Completely changed floorpan shape, dashboard profile, and wiper sizes.

variants.name: 3rd Generation
variants.notes: HEARTECT platform. Flat-bottom steering wheel; completely new bucket seats with high side bolsters.

variants.name: 4th Generation
variants.notes: Latest model. 3D/7D mats, seat profiles, and infotainment screen size completely changed.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Full-sized sedan trunk. Shared front profile and floor structure with 1st Gen Swift.

variants.name: 2nd Generation (Sub-4m)
variants.notes: Compact trunk (<4m). Floor mats differ slightly from Swift due to unique rear legroom styling.

variants.name: 3rd Generation
variants.notes: Dropped 'Swift' prefix. Completely different dashboard layout from Swift 3; unique mat clips.

variants.name: 4th Generation
variants.notes: Distinct blocky styling. Shared interior layout with Swift 4 but distinct seat stitching profiles.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Premium hatchback. Large boot space, 7-inch SmartPlay screen protector fitment.

variants.name: 2nd Generation
variants.notes: Heavy body panel changes. 9-inch screen protector; front seats require airbag-compliant side-seam seat covers.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Diesel only initially, later petrol. Boxy dashboard. Flat, square-ish floor mat layout.

variants.name: 2nd Generation
variants.notes: Dropped 'Vitara'. Sunroof added (impacts roof wrap sizing). Ambient lighting tracks and updated dash profile.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: 7-seater MPV. Shared platform with Swift 2 but distinct long 3-row 3D mat set required.

variants.name: 2nd Generation
variants.notes: Larger body frame. 3rd-row mat design and boot space dimension entirely different from Gen 1.

variants.name: 1st Generation (i10)
variants.notes: Tallboy hatch layout. Dashboard features integrated high-mounted center gear shifter console.

variants.name: 2nd Generation (Grand i10)
variants.notes: Extended wheelbase unique to India. Floorpan and seat dimensions diverge completely from European i10.

variants.name: 3rd Generation (Nios)
variants.notes: Sculpted seats, integrated fixed headrests on lower variants affecting universal seat cover installation.

variants.name: 1st Generation (PB)
variants.notes: Original premium hatch layout. Flat floorboards and standard seat profiles.

variants.name: 2nd Generation (Elite i20)
variants.notes: Completely wide floorpan. Note: Elite Active variant (2015-2020) uses rugged plastic side wheel-arch cladding.

variants.name: 3rd Generation
variants.notes: Low-slung sporty front seats. Large digital cockpit panel screen protector needed.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Flat floor layout in the rear. Bumper chrome garnish and front grille brackets differ between 2015 and 2018.

variants.name: 2nd Generation
variants.notes: Panoramic sunroof version changes roof graphic layouts. 2024 facelift completely changed front chrome and light trims.

variants.name: Fluidic Era (Gen 3/4)
variants.notes: Huge shift in 2011 to Fluidic layout. Floor mats and body covers completely diverge from old model here.

variants.name: Next-Gen Verna (Gen 5)
variants.notes: Ventilated seat options on high variants require specific perforated leatherette seat covers.

variants.name: Futuristic Verna (Gen 6)
variants.notes: Radical fastback design. Longest wheelbase in class; older generation mats will not fit at all.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Original ceramic-trim exterior profile. Standard round steering wheel cover.

variants.name: 1st Gen Facelift
variants.notes: Changed front bumper and added flat-bottom steering wheel layout.

variants.name: 2nd Generation
variants.notes: Phygital look. Twin-spoke illuminated steering wheel cover and custom screen protector for touch HVAC console.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Omega-Arc platform. Large deep footwells for precision 3D/7D mats.

variants.name: 1st Gen Facelift
variants.notes: Full-width LED bars require new light chrome trims. Completely revised center console structure.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Micro-SUV floor mats. Note: Punch.EV (2024+) has a modified central transmission tunnel console.

variants.name: 1st Generation / Classic
variants.notes: Continuous updates. 2014 change altered rear tailgate & panel layout. 2022 renamed to Scorpio Classic.

variants.name: 2nd Generation
variants.notes: Completely distinct global platform. Zero interior or exterior body accessories shared with Scorpio Classic.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Pure off-roader. Canvas top/soft-top replacement structures are key categories.

variants.name: 2nd Generation (3-Door)
variants.notes: Wider lifestyle cabin. Molded plastic interior flooring requiring precision 7D mats with high sidewalls.

variants.name: 2nd Generation (5-Door)
variants.notes: Extended wheelbase. Rear door visors, window curtains, and entirely different full floor mat layouts needed.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Cheetah-inspired body panels. Unique wrap-around seat contours with specific split 3rd-row setup.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Flush door handles (affects anti-scratch film). Dual-screen display requires large panoramic screen guard.

variants.name: 3rd Generation
variants.notes: Arrow-shot form factor. Sleek low-slung cabin layout.

variants.name: 4th Generation
variants.notes: Extended rear legroom. Continued alongside Gen 5 as an entry-level variant for several years.

variants.name: 5th Generation
variants.notes: Longer, wider sedan footprint. Entirely separate generation fitments for mats, body covers, sunshades.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Based on Brio platform. Narrow cabin footprint, simple flat seats.

variants.name: 2nd Generation
variants.notes: Boxy design language. Square footwells and significantly deeper dashboard panel.

variants.name: 1st Generation / Type 1-4
variants.notes: Immense variant changes across years affecting chrome garnish, front grilles, and light trims.

variants.name: Innova Crysta
variants.notes: Premium MPV structure. Captain seat vs 7-seater configurations radically alter center row seat covers.

variants.name: Innova Hycross
variants.notes: Monocoque TNGA-C platform. Completely flat second-row floor pan (no transmission tunnel bump like Crysta).

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Classic rugged layout. Chrome trim moldings on light blocks differ heavily before/after 2012 facelift.

variants.name: 2nd Generation
variants.notes: Legender and GR-S variants have completely custom front bumper lips and grille assemblies for chrome kits.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Standard floorpans and separate binnacle infotainment screen housing frame.

variants.name: 1st Gen Facelift
variants.notes: Connected dual panoramic screen guards. Completely re-profiled front bumper & lower skid plate fittings.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Compact under-4m SUV setup. High center tunnel console between front seats.

variants.name: 1st Gen Facelift
variants.notes: Sleek C-shaped rear light connectivity requiring custom chrome appliques; modified adjustable rear headrest tracks.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Timeless frame layout but minor front lip bumper alterations across updates. Floor pan dimensions stayed identical.

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: MQB-A0-IN platform. Front seats have side-airbags standard (critical note for specialized seat covers).

variants.name: 1st Generation
variants.notes: Sister car to VW Taigun but uses a fully distinct dashboard design and center console layout.
